title: Drain in-flight work on shutdown
description: On SIGTERM the service stops accepting new work, finishes or re-queues in-flight items, then exits within the grace period.
body: Use when a worker or server can be redeployed, scaled down, or evicted. Do not exit immediately on SIGTERM while jobs are mid-flight or rely on the orchestrator's kill. Actions: trap SIGTERM, stop accepting new work, wait for in-flight handlers up to a deadline shorter than the orchestrator grace, then nack/re-queue anything unfinished. Validation signals include a test that sends SIGTERM under load and asserts no job is lost and acks happen only after completion. Risk boundary: dropping in-flight work on deploy silently loses user actions and is a reliability defect.
